tactical_shape: more proactive approach with Alphonso Davies back, using him as an outlet out wide to aid build-up play
kept their block of possession in Qatar's half and pressed/pinned Qatar back from the start
crowd-driven high-pressure approach with an expected 12th-man atmosphere
standout_players: Alphonso Davies — Canada’s best player; return was expected to help build-up play
Jonathan David — hat-trick in the 6-0 win
Ismaël Koné — helped pull the strings in midfield, but suffered a horror leg injury in the second half
Stephen Eustáquio — named as one of the midfielders to pull the strings
Cyle Larin — scored the opening breakthrough after Qatar failed to clear Ali Ahmed’s service
Ali Ahmed — delivered the service for the first goal
fatigue_injuries: Ismaël Koné suffered a horror leg injury in the second half
home_read.momentum: Historic first men’s World Cup win, with a dominant performance and strong belief they can reach the knockout round.

tactical_shape: familiar with Concacaf travel and the physical style typically associated with Concacaf soccer
were pinned back for long spells and only briefly found balance after an Akram Afif break
standout_players: Akram Afif — produced a scamper and won a foul to help Qatar briefly settle
away_read.momentum: A heavy 6-0 defeat leaves them overpowered and searching for a response after being outclassed in Vancouver.
